title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: You have just finished a three-week work placement at an international company. Your manager has asked you to write a report describing the tasks you performed, evaluating the usefulness of the experience, and suggesting how the placement programme could be improved.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Work Placement at Global Tech Solutions

Introduction
The aim of this report is to evaluate my recent three-week work placement at Global Tech Solutions. It outlines the responsibilities I held, the benefits of the experience, and recommendations for future interns.

Work Performed
During my tenure, I was primarily assigned to the marketing department. My duties included conducting market research for the new 'Eco-Line' product range and drafting social media content. Additionally, I assisted the project manager in coordinating weekly team briefings and updating the client database.

Evaluation of Experience
The placement proved to be immensely beneficial. It provided me with a practical understanding of how international marketing strategies are implemented. Furthermore, working in a fast-paced corporate environment allowed me to hone my communication and time-management skills. The opportunity to shadow senior executives during board meetings was particularly insightful, offering a rare glimpse into high-level decision-making processes.

Suggestions for Improvement
While the experience was largely positive, I believe the programme could be enhanced by:
- Assigning a dedicated mentor to each intern for more consistent feedback.
- Providing a more structured induction session on the first day to explain company software.
- Including interns in cross-departmental projects to provide a broader overview of the company.

Conclusion
Overall, the placement was a rewarding professional milestone. Implementing the aforementioned suggestions would ensure that future participants derive even greater value from the programme.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt: You have recently stayed at a hotel that was highly recommended by a friend, but you were very disappointed with the service. Write a letter to the hotel manager explaining your complaints and asking for a refund.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Mr. Henderson,

I am writing to formally express my extreme dissatisfaction with my recent stay at the Grand Plaza Hotel from March 10th to 12th. I had chosen your establishment based on a glowing recommendation, but unfortunately, my experience was far from satisfactory.

My primary grievance concerns the state of the room. Upon arrival, I discovered that the air conditioning was non-functional. Despite reporting this immediately, no technician arrived until the following morning, resulting in a very uncomfortable night. Furthermore, the cleanliness of the bathroom left much to be desired, with evidence of previous guests still visible.

To make matters worse, the service at your restaurant was unacceptably slow. On the second evening, I waited over an hour for a simple main course, and when it finally arrived, it was cold. When I raised this with the head waiter, his attitude was dismissive rather than apologetic, which I found particularly insulting.

As a frequent traveller, I expect a much higher standard of professionalism and hygiene from a four-star hotel. Given that my stay was thoroughly ruined by these oversights, I believe it is only fair that I receive a full refund for the cost of the accommodation.

I look forward to receiving your prompt response regarding this matter.

Yours sincerely,
Julian Thorne

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen an advertisement in an English-language magazine: 'We are looking for reviews of local restaurants that offer something unique. Tell us about the atmosphere, the food, and why it stands out from other places.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
A Culinary Journey: The Garden Attic

Tucked away on the top floor of an old industrial warehouse, 'The Garden Attic' is a hidden gem that defies the traditional dining experience. I recently had the pleasure of visiting this establishment, and it is undoubtedly one of the most unique eateries in the city.

The first thing that strikes you is the atmosphere. The owners have transformed a stark, urban space into a lush indoor forest. With real trees growing between tables and the sound of a distant waterfall, you feel instantly transported away from the city's hustle and bustle. It provides a sense of tranquillity that is rare in modern dining.

However, the true star is the menu. The chef focuses exclusively on 'forgotten ingredients'—wild plants and heritage vegetables that have fallen out of fashion. I opted for the roasted dandelion root risotto, which was a revelation of earthy, complex flavours. Every dish is presented as a work of art, adorned with edible flowers and served on handcrafted stone plates. This commitment to unconventional ingredients sets it apart from the dozens of identical Italian and French bistros in the area.

While the prices are somewhat higher than average, the sheer creativity and the magical environment make it worth every penny. For anyone tired of predictable menus and seeking a sensory adventure, 'The Garden Attic' is an absolute must-visit. It is not just a meal; it is an unforgettable experience.
